frameworks.name: category prototype vs boundary encoding
frameworks.one_line: Within-category variability indexed by distance from category boundary

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: RSA used to test for cross-category information in cortical regions

frameworks.name: hierarchical cortical categorization network
frameworks.one_line: Visual and frontal regions form a hierarchy for active color categorization

core_question: How is fine-grained color perception mapped onto discrete categories across the cortical hierarchy?
key_assumptions: Color stimuli can be parameterized by distance from the category boundary
Active categorization task engages category-relevant neural processes
fMRI BOLD modulation reflects category-specific encoding
manipulations: Varied stimulus distance from category boundary (near vs far)
Contrasted within-category (prototype vs boundary) and cross-category differences
key_findings: Frontal areas (IFG, MFG, medial SFG, insula) responded more to near-boundary colors
Left V1 in calcarine showed greater responses for category-center colors than far-from-boundary
Bilateral V4 enhanced for both category-center and around-boundary colors
RSA showed bilateral insulae and V4a carry cross-category information
interpretations: Color categorization is supported by a hierarchically organized cortical network
Frontal and visual cortices play complementary roles in boundary vs prototype encoding
